articles.title: Masked fan unplugs VAR monitor in German second division match
articles.summary: A masked fan unplugged a VAR monitor in a German second division match as the referee went to the pitchside screen to check a penalty award.

articles.title: Mourinho sent off after late Benfica equaliser
articles.summary: Benfica manager Jose Mourinho is shown a red card following an altercation between the benches after his side score a late equaliser against his former club FC Porto.

articles.title: Huddersfield 6-32 Hull KR
articles.summary: Highlights of the Super League match between Huddersfield Giants and Hull KR.

articles.title: Gauff injures left arm as she retires and bows out of Indian Wells Open
articles.summary: Highlights from the Indian Wells Open match between Coco Gauff and Alexandra Eala.

articles.title: 'Wrong reason, right decision' - Why referee blew whistle to deny Fulham goal
articles.summary: Match of the Day's Ellen White and Glenn Murray analyse why referee Jarred Gillett blew his whistle to deny Fulham a goal in their 1-0 FA Cup defeat to Southampton.

articles.title: League One side Port Vale stun Sunderland in FA Cup upset
articles.summary: League One side Port Vale knock out Premier League team Sunderland to reach the FA Cup quarter-finals for the first time since 1954.

articles.title: Southampton knock out Fulham with stoppage-time penalty
articles.summary: Southampton's Ross Stewart scores a penalty in stoppage time against Fulham which sends the Championship side to the FA Cup quarter-finals.

articles.title: Teenage Oxford United player dies during academy match
articles.summary: The 15-year-old Oxford United academy player collapsed during a game against Fulham.

articles.title: The anatomy of a corner-kick: how set-pieces reshaped the Premier League
articles.summary: As corner kicks continue to cause chaos in the Premier League, BBC Sport take a closer at why they are becoming so hard to defend.

articles.title: St Mirren beat Partick Thistle to reach Scottish Cup semi-finals
articles.summary: Goals from Dan Nlundulu and Jake Young helped St Mirren defeat Partick Thistle 2-1 to book their place in the Scottish Cup semi-finals.

articles.title: 'What happens next, happens' - Lindsay coy on NI future
articles.summary: Northern Ireland interim manager Kris Lindsay says it is "up to the powers that be" at the Irish FA to decide whether he will be appointed as permanent boss.

articles.title: Bayern hammer Monchengladbach to move 14 points clear
articles.summary: Bayern Munich win their fifth match in a row with a 4-1 win over Borussia Monchengladbach, to move to 14 points clear at the top of the Bundesliga table.

articles.title: Early goals send Falkirk to Scottish Cup semi-finals
articles.summary: Two early goals help Falkirk make it by Dundee United to reach the Scottish Cup semi-finals.

articles.title: 'As pure as you can hit it!' Robertson fires Liverpool into lead
articles.summary: Liverpool left-back Andrew Robertson scores a superb opening goal for his side in their FA Cup fifth round tie against Wolves at Molineux.

articles.title: Pressure mounts on Tudor as Tottenham lose at home to Palace
articles.summary: Tottenham stay just one point above the Premier League relegation zone following a 3-1 home defeat by Crystal Palace, having had to play more than 45 minutes with 10 men after Micky van de Ven was sen...

articles.title: Leeds ease past Norwich to reach FA Cup quarter-finals
articles.summary: Leeds reach the FA Cup quarter-finals for the first time in over 20 years, after comfortably beating Championship side Norwich 3-0 at Elland Road.

articles.title: Sivo hat-trick takes Leeds to Castleford victory as Hull KR bounce back
articles.summary: Maika Sivo scored a hat-trick as Leeds claimed a 22-4 Betfred Super League win over Castleford.

articles.title: Leeds power past Norwich to reach first FA Cup quarter-final since 2003
articles.summary: Leeds reached their first FA Cup quarter-final since 2003 after easing past Championship side Norwich with a 3-0 victory at Elland Road.

articles.title: Bottom of the league, magic in the cup - Port Vale's 'incredible' run
articles.summary: Port Vale are bottom of League One, but they have reached the FA Cup quarter-finals for only the second time in their history.

articles.title: Celtic v St Mirren and Falkirk v Dunfermline derby in Scottish Cup semis
articles.summary: Falkirk and Dunfermline Athletic will meet in a Scottish Cup semi-final derby at Hampden next month, while Celtic will face St Mirren in a repeat of this season's League Cup final.
